title: Stars
content:
How countlessly they congregate
O'er our tumultuous snow,
Which flows in shapes as tall as trees
When wintry winds do blow!

As if with keenness for our fate,
Our faltering few steps on
To white rest, and a place of rest
Invisible at dawn,

And yet with neither love nor hate,
Those starts like some snow-white
Minerva's snow-white marble eyes
Without the gift of sight.
meaning:
Robert Lee Frost's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Stars"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
